title: Add is_active column to players table
description: Create a database migration to add an is_active boolean column to the players table, defaulting to TRUE. This is the foundation for soft-delete: inactive players are excluded from current operations but their historical data (match results, past season memberships) is preserved. Add an index on is_active for efficient filtering.
acceptance_criteria: New migration adds is_active BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE to players table
Index idx_players_is_active created on the new column
Down migration removes the column and index
All existing players default to is_active = TRUE after migration
Player model struct updated with IsActive field
Migration runs cleanly on existing database with data

approach: 1. Determine next migration number from existing migrations
2. Create up migration: ALTER TABLE players ADD COLUMN is_active BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE
3. Create index: CREATE INDEX idx_players_is_active ON players(is_active)
5. Add IsActive bool field to Player struct in models.go
6. Verify migration runs on existing database without errors

testing_requirements: Migration applies cleanly to database with existing player data
All existing players have is_active = TRUE after migration
Player model correctly maps the new field

context.user_story: As a system administrator, I need a way to mark players as inactive without destroying their historical match data
